name: Ribollita (Tuscan Vegetable & Bread Soup)
recipeIngredient: 19 oz White kidney beans, drained and rinsed
8 slices French or Italian bread, stale
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, bring chicken stock, chicken breasts, and bay leaves to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for about 20 minutes, or until the chicken is no longer pink inside. Remove the chicken and discard the bones. Dice the meat and set aside. Discard the bay leaves. Keep the stock warm.
2. Meanwhile, in a large skillet, heat 2 tbsp of olive oil over medium heat. Cook chopped onion, garlic, diced carrots, and celery for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add the remaining oil, diced green pepper, chopped cabbage, thyme, and rosemary. Cook over low heat, stirring occasionally for 10 minutes.
3. Add the vegetable mixture to the stock in the saucepan, along with tomatoes and pepper. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes.
4. Add spinach, parsley, zucchini, kidney beans, and reserved diced chicken. Cook for 5 minutes. Remove 1 cup of soup and set aside.
5. Ladle half of the remaining soup into a Dutch oven or casserole dish. Cover with 4 bread slices and 1/2 cup Parmesan cheese. Cover with the remaining soup, layering with the remaining bread. Drizzle reserved soup over top and sprinkle with the remaining Parmesan cheese.
6. (Recipe can be prepared to this point, cooled, covered, and refrigerated for up to 24 hours.) Bake, covered in a 350°F oven for 20 minutes (45 minutes if refrigerated). Uncover and bake for an additional 20 minutes (45 minutes if refrigerated) or until hot.
7. Garnish: Ladle soup into large warmed bowls. Garnish with a drizzle of olive oil and sprinkle of freshly grated Parmesan cheese.
